seniority.level: Senior IC (Mid-Senior, likely Senior Manager or IC5 equivalent)
level_signals: 4-7 years of experience in partner management, program management, account management, or a related commercial operations role
Comp range $300K-$355K base, consistent with Senior IC / Senior Manager at Anthropic
Owns operational workflows and reports to Marketplace leadership

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 4-7 years of experience in partner management, program management, account management, or a related commercial operations role at a technology company
A track record of managing multiple external relationships simultaneously while keeping internal stakeholders aligned and informed
Experience running programs across organizational boundaries where you had no direct authority over most of the people whose work you depended on, and delivered anyway
Strong organizational skills and a demonstrated ability to build and improve processes in fast-moving, ambiguous environments
Experience working closely with sales or GTM teams, with a feel for how partnerships translate into customer value and commercial outcomes
Clear, concise communication skills — both written and verbal — and comfort engaging with senior stakeholders
A collaborative, low-ego working style and willingness to take on whatever the team needs, from strategic analysis to day-to-day coordination
Comfort with ambiguity and shifting priorities in a high-growth environment where you'll often need to exercise judgment with incomplete information
Comfort being the person who notices when something is broken before it shows up in a number, and the judgment to know how to triage versus design for the future
Bachelor's degree or an equivalent combination of education, training, and/or experience

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience in the enterprise SaaS or developer platform ecosystem, particularly in roles involving technology partnerships or integrations
Familiarity with AI/ML products and how enterprises are adopting AI into their technology stacks
A background in consulting, business operations, or strategy roles that involved both analytical work and cross-functional coordination
Experience at a high-growth technology company during a period of rapid scaling, particularly in a role that required building new programs or processes from the ground up
Experience operating a two-sided marketplace, app store, or platform partner program with a transaction or revenue-share component
Enough technical fluency to be a credible partner to product and engineering on integration and data-flow questions

scope.surface: Claude Marketplace — a platform connecting Claude-powered enterprise tools through technology partnerships and customer relationships
scope.business_model: enterprise B2B platform/marketplace with transaction and revenue-share components
core_responsibilities: Own the marketplace transaction end-to-end across BD, Legal, Accounting, Finance, Product, and Engineering
Fill operational gaps as the program scales — managing data in spreadsheets, triaging partner or customer requests, standing up lightweight processes
Own and continuously improve operational processes including partner and customer onboarding, billing, reporting, and cross-functional coordination
Translate ground-level insights into product and policy requirements that inform the Marketplace roadmap
Design and build operational scaffolding for the program; monitor and report on program health and throughput to Marketplace leadership

compensation.equity_form: optional equity donation matching mentioned (suggests options/RSUs are part of package)

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and highly competitive ($300K-$355K base)
Clear ownership scope: the Marketplace transaction and program operations end-to-end
Role bridges commercial and product/engineering work — broad and interesting mandate
Encourages applications even from candidates who don't meet every qualification
Explicit acknowledgment of building in ambiguity — signals entrepreneurial culture
red_flags: Hybrid policy requires at least 25% office time in San Francisco — eliminates fully-remote candidates
Compensation range labeled as 'Annual Salary' but JD preamble mentions OTE for sales roles — unclear if any variable/commission component applies here; treat as base salary but worth confirming
Role is broad and blends program ops, program management, and product management — scope may be undefined or shifting in early-stage program
4-7 years range is wide, suggesting internal leveling may vary significantly

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range + years range + responsibilities)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly in JD)
location.cities_listed (San Francisco inferred from HQ reference; no other cities named for this role)
equity_form (inferred from company benefits boilerplate mentioning optional equity donation matching)
ambiguities: Compensation preamble states that for sales roles the range is OTE (base + commissions/bonuses), implying non-sales roles have base-only ranges. This role is Ops/Strategy, not sales, so $300K-$355K is treated as base salary. However, the boundary is not 100% explicit — worth confirming with recruiter.
JD does not explicitly name San Francisco as the required city for this role; the HQ address is mentioned in closing. Treated as the primary office location.
